title: BBC Knew About DJ Investigation Years Ago
summary: The BBC, a big TV and radio company, knew about a police investigation into DJ Scott Mills in 2017. Scott Mills was a popular DJ on Radio 2. The police were looking into old claims of serious bad behavior from 1997 to 2000, but no charges were made because there wasn't enough evidence. Recently, the BBC got new information about Mills and fired him in March 2024. The BBC said they are working to improve how they handle such situations. Mills has not commented on this news. The BBC's boss at the time did not know about the investigation. This story shows how companies deal with problems and try to do the right thing.

background_read: The BBC is a well-known media organization in the UK that broadcasts TV and radio. DJs like Scott Mills play music and entertain listeners. Sometimes, police investigate people if there are claims of wrongdoing, but they need evidence to take action. Companies like the BBC have rules to follow and may fire employees if they break those rules. This story helps us understand how big organizations respond to serious issues.
Article_Structure: WHO: The BBC and DJ Scott Mills
WHAT: The BBC knew about a police investigation in 2017 and fired Mills in 2024 after new information
WHERE: In the UK, involving the BBC and police
WHY: To address claims of bad behavior and improve the BBC's culture

perspectives.description: The BBC says they acted decisively to fire Mills and are working to improve their processes.
People might be shocked and want more answers about what happened.
